truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2: 5 findings reported; 4 correctly identified true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001, SC-V3-004, SC-V3-007, SC-V3-009), 1 overclaimed (Arbitrary Command Execution via admin exec). All other listed vulnerabilities were missed. No false positives aside from the overclaim.

Items.Notes: Exact match; evidence lines 237-238 show printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input). Severity correctly identified as High.
Source contains factorial implementation (lines 312-320) with result *= i and no overflow protection. Not mentioned in previous answer.
Deferred history flush allocates scratch buffer (line 818), sleeps (823), then uses it; memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup runs afterwards, creating use-after-free. Not in previous answer.
Previous answer correctly identified validator_cmd + config_file concatenated into system() call. Severity High matches true listing.
Input processing uses strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()) at line 831 without bounds check. Not mentioned.
Thread-pool counter uses volatile long long and lock_guard; increment sequence is not atomic. Race condition exists but was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Weak Authentication Bypass via Emergency Override
Items.Notes: Correct identification of EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE check that bypasses authentication after 5 failed attempts. Severity High matches.
ConfigLoader concatenates config_dir_ + filename without path validation, enabling traversal. Not mentioned.
ExpressionParser uses a 256-byte buffer (buffer_size_) and strcpy(expr_str.c_str()) at line 376. This is the heap overflow described; severity High matches.
Hardcoded ADMIN_SECRET = SC_ENT_2025_AUTH in config::security namespace. Not mentioned.
Session token generator uses std::mt19937 seeded only by random_device; insufficient entropy for strong session tokens. Not mentioned.
log_debug_message uses static std::tm cached_time without synchronization across threads. Race exists but unreported.
Temporary file handling in append_override_audit_event writes via system() without atomicity, vulnerable to race. Not mentioned.
Persistent authentication state: AdminConsole stores authenticated_ and login_attempts_ globally, allowing state persistence across sessions. Not identified.
handle_variable_command calls std::stod(tokens[3]) without verifying token is numeric, risking exception. Not mentioned.
Memory-pool split calculation previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow when size is large. Not reported.
ExpressionParser recursion has no depth limit; unbounded nesting of parentheses can cause stack overflow. Not mentioned.
result_cache_ is accessed in evaluate_cached without any mutex protection, creating a data race. Not identified.
load_config checks std::filesystem::exists then sleeps 25ms before opening file, classic TOCTOU race. Not reported.
calculate_route_score divides 100 by token_length without checking for zero, leading to division-by-zero. Not mentioned.

Items.Key: arbitrary command execution via admin exec|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|607|execute_admin_command| if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|any os command can be executed with full administrative privileges.|an authenticated admin (or attacker who bypassed authentication) issues `admin exec <command>`.|validate and whitelist all commands before passing to system(), or replace with execve() with strict argument checks.->arbitrary command execution via admin exec|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|execute_admin_command| if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|any os command can be executed with full administrative privileges.|an authenticated admin (or attacker who bypassed authentication) issues `admin exec <command>`.|validate and whitelist all commands before passing to system(), or replace with execve() with strict argument checks.
